body.title: Eliminate IGOR_HOME env var — UU_ROOT is the only canonical env var; data dir via resolver
body.intention: I intend that UU_ROOT is the ONE canonical env var; the runtime data dir (~/.unseen_university) is derived, not configured via IGOR_HOME.
body.description:
**Problem (Akien 2026-06-24):** two canonical env vars is one too many. UU_ROOT stays; IGOR_HOME goes away. The runtime data dir is always ~/.unseen_university — make it a derived resolver, not a configurable env var. **Shape:** add `uu_home()` to unseen_university (sibling of `uu_root()`; returns ~/.unseen_university; NO env var) and replace the 18 python `os.environ[...IGOR_HOME...]` / `${IGOR_HOME:-...}` reads with it. For shell contexts (bin/uu_bash_profile_processor.sh, ~/bin/_igor_common.sh, the ground_loop.service unit) use `$HOME/.unseen_university` directly and drop the IGOR_HOME export/Environment line. Update CLAUDE.md: 'Two canonical env vars' -> ONE (UU_ROOT); IGOR_HOME row -> 'derived via uu_home(), not an env var'. **Affected files:** unseen_university/_uu_root.py (or sibling — add uu_home()); 18 py readers; bin/uu_bash_profile_processor.sh; ~/bin/_igor_common.sh; ground_loop.service unit; CLAUDE.md. **Design rules:** mirror uu_root() resolver pattern; CP6 recovery-safe (resolver never errors). **Scope boundary:** IN — the resolver + IGOR_HOME removal + CLAUDE.md contract. OUT — the env-file mechanism (T-uu-env-file-for-noninteractive — which now carries one fewer var). **Test plan:** add unseen_university; grep shows zero live IGOR_HOME env reads (resolver only); uu_home() returns ~/.unseen_university with IGOR_HOME unset (red before: code depended on the env var/default). **Completion criteria:** no live code or unit depends on an IGOR_HOME env var; uu_home() is the single source for the data dir; CLAUDE.md documents one canonical env var.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

## 2026-06-24 — Akien architecture context + test rule (re-sized M->L)
WHY IGOR_HOME existed: single-repo era, when Igor was the sole occupant and there was no bus/MCP/rack. Obsolete now. The only real 'Igor home' is (a) his device in the rack for CODE (devices/igor/), and (b) COMING CHANGE — per-instance runtime at ~/.unseen_university/devices/igor/<IGOR_INSTANCE_ID>/ (everything for that igor EXCEPT the database and logs). That devices/igor/<instance> restructure is a SEPARATE upcoming ticket — NOT this one. Here, uu_home() = ~/.unseen_university (the data root); Igor's instance dir keeps using _igor_paths().instance (absorbs the restructure later).

RESOLVER DESIGN: uu_home() is env-free AND read at CALL TIME (not an import-time module constant), so the test seam works by monkeypatching uu_home(). Converting import-time `_IGOR_HOME = Path(os.environ.get(...))` constants to call-time uu_home() is the real care in this ticket.

TEST RULE (Akien): for each IGOR_HOME test ask 'rack functionality -> update; programming-from-within-igor -> obsolete, delete.' TRIAGE of the 16: ALL are rack/infra isolation-seam tests (vetinari x3+, system_alarms x3, inference, queue/cost_to_close, skill_telemetry x2, demo_daemon, web_alarms_panel) -> UPDATE the seam (setenv IGOR_HOME -> monkeypatch uu_home). NONE are igor-self-programming -> zero deletions. EXCEPTION: test_context_load_violation_summary is owned by T-context-load-violation-summary-missing (pre-existing broken) - do NOT touch here.

FULL SCOPE: (1) add uu_home() to unseen_university/_uu_root.py; (2) 16 source readers -> uu_home() (call-time) / igor shim+device -> _igor_paths().instance (drop env override); (3) ~15 test seam updates; (4) CLAUDE.md two->one canonical env var; (5) bin/uu_bash_profile_processor.sh + ~/bin/_igor_common.sh + ground_loop.service unit drop IGOR_HOME. EXCLUDE (different vars): IGOR_HOME_SEARCH_PATH, IGOR_HOMEOSTATIC_*.
body.result: Eliminated the IGOR_HOME env var. uu_home() (env-free, call-time, ~/.unseen_university) replaces it across 30 source readers; igor shim/device -> _igor_paths().instance. 18 test isolation seams moved setenv(IGOR_HOME) -> monkeypatch(uu_home), all rack/infra (none deleted). CLAUDE.md two->one canonical env var; processor + ground_loop units drop IGOR_HOME. Proof: data dir ignores a set IGOR_HOME env. Excludes distinct IGOR_HOME_SEARCH_PATH/IGOR_HOMEOSTATIC_*; devices/igor/<instance> restructure is a separate ticket.
